diseases.label: Melanoma
diseases.description: Melanoma is the most serious type of skin cancer. It develops in the cells (melanocytes) that produce melanin—the pigment that gives your skin its color.
diseases.symptoms: A change in an existing mole or the development of a new, unusual-looking growth on the skin.
diseases.advice: See a dermatologist immediately. Do not delay. This requires specialized biopsy and treatment.
diseases.moroccan_context: Daba darori khassek tmchi l tbib dyal jled f akrab wakt. Melnouma hya as3ab naw3 dyal saratan l jeld.

diseases.label: Melanocytic nevus
diseases.description: A melanocytic nevus (also known as a mole) is a common, noncancerous skin growth of pigment-producing cells.
diseases.symptoms: Small, dark brown spots caused by clusters of pigmented cells.
diseases.advice: Usually harmless. However, monitor for changes in size, shape, or color (ABCDE criteria).
diseases.moroccan_context: Hadi ghir chama 3adiya. Walakin dima bka t9lebha ila bdat t9bare awla tbedel loun dyalha.

diseases.label: Basal cell carcinoma
diseases.description: Basal cell carcinoma (BCC) is a type of skin cancer that begins in the basal cells. It often appears as a slightly transparent bump on the skin.
diseases.symptoms: A pearly white, skin-colored, or pink bump that is translucent, meaning you can see a bit through the surface.
diseases.advice: Highly treatable and rarely spreads, but needs surgical removal to prevent local tissue damage.
diseases.moroccan_context: Hada naw3 dyal saratan l jeld li kikon ghaliban mzyan ila t3alej bekri. Khasso l'operation bach ithiyed.

diseases.label: Actinic keratosis
diseases.description: Actinic keratosis (AK) is a rough, scaly patch on your skin that develops from years of exposure to the sun.
diseases.symptoms: Rough, dry, or scaly patch of skin, usually less than 1 inch (2.5 centimeters) in diameter.
diseases.advice: Considered precancerous. If left untreated, it can develop into squamous cell carcinoma.
diseases.moroccan_context: Hadi b9a3 sghira kadji mn lberd awla chemch. Darori t3alejha hit t9der tbedel l mard khayeb m3a lwa9t.

diseases.label: Benign keratosis-like lesions
diseases.description: These are noncancerous skin growths that can include seborrheic keratoses, which appear as waxy, scaly, and slightly elevated growths.
diseases.symptoms: Waxy or wart-like growths that look like they've been pasted on the skin.
diseases.advice: Benign and harmless. No treatment is medically necessary unless for cosmetic reasons.
diseases.moroccan_context: Hadi afa jildiya hamida, ma3ndha tati khoutoura. Ma khassekch tkhli3 mnha.

diseases.label: Dermatofibroma
diseases.description: Dermatofibromas are common noncancerous (benign) skin growths. They are typically firm, red-to-brown bumps.
diseases.symptoms: A firm bump that can sometimes feel like a pea under the skin.
diseases.advice: Harmless. Usually ignored unless it causes pain or irritation.
diseases.moroccan_context: Hada kora sghira 9asha7 f l jeld. Hamida o ma fiha hta khoutoura.

diseases.label: Vascular lesions
diseases.description: Vascular lesions are relatively common abnormalities of the skin and underlying tissues, more commonly known as birthmarks or spider veins.
diseases.symptoms: Red, purple, or blue marks caused by abnormal blood vessels.
diseases.advice: Usually benign. Can be treated with laser for cosmetic reasons.
diseases.moroccan_context: Hadi wahmat damawiya. Ghaliban hamida o katkon mn lwilada awla katban m3a lkber.

diseases.label: CNV
diseases.description: Choroidal Neovascularization (CNV) is the creation of new blood vessels in the choroid layer of the eye, often associated with age-related macular degeneration.
diseases.symptoms: Blurred vision, distorted vision, or a dark spot in the central vision.
diseases.advice: Urgent consultation with an ophthalmologist is required. Treatments include anti-VEGF injections.
diseases.moroccan_context: Hada mard f l'عين ki aser 3la choufa dyalk. Khassek tmchi l tbib dyal l'3inine darori bach tdir t9ari.

diseases.label: DME
diseases.description: Diabetic Macular Edema (DME) is an accumulation of fluid in the macula part of the retina that controls our most detailed vision.
diseases.symptoms: Blurred or wavy vision near or in the center of the field of vision.
diseases.advice: Control your blood sugar levels strictly. Regular eye exams are critical for diabetic patients.
diseases.moroccan_context: Hada mard ki dji l nass li fihom sokar. Darori tkon mtab3 mzyan m3a tbib dyal sokar o dyal l'3inine.

diseases.label: DRUSEN
diseases.description: Drusen are yellow deposits under the retina. They are made up of lipids and proteins and are common as people age.
diseases.symptoms: Usually asymptomatic until they grow larger, leading to vision distortion.
diseases.advice: Monitor vision using an Amsler grid. Regular check-ups to detect early stages of Macular Degeneration.
diseases.moroccan_context: Hadi b9a3 sghira sffra katban mn t7et chabakiya. Katji m3a l kber f l3oumre.

diseases.label: Normal
diseases.description: The retinal tissue appears healthy with no visible signs of CNV, DME, or Drusen.
diseases.symptoms: Clear, unobstructed vision suitable for the patient's age.
diseases.advice: Maintain healthy lifestyle and routine annual eye checkups.
diseases.moroccan_context: Kolchi mzyan, l'3in dyalk bayna n9iya o ma fiha walou.
